_meta.purpose: Pinned offline regression baseline for class-term / brand-list completeness.
_meta.complementsExisting: test-drug-brand-coverage.js guards rules/drug-rules.json drug.match via real drugMatchesRule(); this file guards the hand-maintained JS term tables in engine/stopp-start.js, engine/acb-scores.js, and content-scripts/triage-lens/content.js — the other hand that has no guard and has already drifted.
_meta.updatePolicy: Add rows here when you extend a term list in product code (Wave C canonicalisation). Never remove rows — they are regression sentinels. Each class has a source comment field documenting provenance.

stopp_nsaid._source: UK BNF 10.1.1 / MHRA NSAID class / PINCER rules (alert-library.json). As of Wave C (3.114), engine/stopp-start.js NSAID_TERMS is brought to parity with the content.js NSAIDS regex / alert-library: the full UK generic set + major brands. The previously-stranded generics (piroxicam, aceclofenac, nabumetone, mefenamic acid) are now covered and locked into mustMatchAll.
stopp_nsaid._matchMechanism: computeStoppStart() hasDrug() — case-insensitive substring of drug.label

mustMatchAll.note: Wave C: added to NSAID_TERMS (was a silent gap)
Wave C: UK spelling in NSAID_TERMS
Wave C: US/legacy spelling also in NSAID_TERMS
UK ibuprofen brand — present in NSAID_TERMS
UK diclofenac brand — present in NSAID_TERMS
UK etoricoxib brand — present in NSAID_TERMS
UK meloxicam brand — present in NSAID_TERMS

stopp_aspirin_lowdose._source: BNF 2.9 (antiplatelet drugs). UK low-dose aspirin forms: generic dm+d names, Nu-seals (AstraZeneca), Caprin (Pinewood), Micropirin (M&A Pharmachem). The aspirin STOPP check (stopp_aspirin_primary_prev) requires matching low-dose antiplatelet forms without catching analgesic doses. As of Wave C (3.114) ASPIRIN_TERMS adds the gastro-resistant word-order variant and the three UK 75mg brands; the previous known gaps are now locked into mustMatchAll.
stopp_aspirin_lowdose._matchMechanism: computeStoppStart() hasDrug() — case-insensitive substring of drug.label against ASPIRIN_TERMS

mustMatchAll.note: Wave C: matches via 'aspirin gastro' (word-order variant)
Wave C: UK AZ aspirin 75mg brand — matches via 'nu-seals'
Wave C: Pinewood aspirin 75mg brand — matches via 'caprin'
Wave C: M&A aspirin 75mg brand — matches via 'micropirin'

acb_scores_score3._source: Boustani ACB scale (ACBcalc.com). Score 3 = strong/definite anticholinergic. Source file: engine/acb-scores.js ACB_TABLE entries with score:3.

acb_scores_score2._source: Boustani ACB scale. Score 2 = moderate anticholinergic. Smaller set on the Boustani list.

content_js_anticholinergic._source: content-scripts/triage-lens/content.js in-queue ACB scoring. As of Wave C (3.114) content.js NO LONGER carries a separate hardcoded ANTICHOLINERGIC table — it delegates to the SINGLE canonical scorer window.ACBScores.computeACB (engine/acb-scores.js), now loaded ahead of content.js in the manifest content_scripts list. The queue HUD and the record/engine view therefore score identically by construction; the divergences below are RESOLVED and kept only as regression sentinels.
content_js_anticholinergic._matchMechanism: content.js calls window.ACBScores.computeACB(allMeds) — same engine, same SORTED_TABLE, per-drug additive sum.

resolvedDivergences.note: dosulepin added to acb-scores.js so the (now sole) engine path scores it 3; previously it scored 3 only in the queue and 0 in the record.
single scorer now returns 1 everywhere (quaternary, limited CNS penetration — the defensible value).
no longer under-scored in the queue.
no longer over-scored in the queue.

_droppedFromContentJs.note: content.js previously scored these as anticholinergic; they are NOT on the Boustani/ACBcalc scale in engine/acb-scores.js and were dropped as likely false positives when the queue switched to the single scorer. (codeine/furosemide/prednisolone were ALSO scored by content.js but ARE on the engine's score-1 list, so they continue to score 1 — not dropped.) Recorded for audit so a future change that re-adds the dropped terms is a deliberate, reviewed act.
